description: ISO/IEC 27001 is an international standard for information security management systems (ISMS). This domain knowledge provides role-based guidance and use cases for processing ISO27001 compliance-related data questions.
detailed_domain_knowledge: ISO/IEC 27001 is an international standard that specifies requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an Information Security Management System (ISMS). The standard follows a risk-based approach, requiring organizations to identify information security risks and implement appropriate controls to mitigate those risks. ISO27001 is part of the ISO/IEC 27000 family of standards, with ISO/IEC 27002 providing detailed guidance on information security controls. The standard uses a Plan-Do-Check-Act (PDCA) cycle for continuous improvement. Key components include: context of the organization (understanding internal and external issues, interested parties, scope), leadership (top management commitment, roles and responsibilities, policy), planning (risk assessment, risk treatment, objectives, planning of changes), support (resources, competence, awareness, communication, documented information), operation (operational planning and control, information security risk assessment, information security risk treatment), performance evaluation (monitoring, measurement, analysis, evaluation, internal audit, management review), and improvement (nonconformity and corrective action, continual improvement). The standard includes Annex A with 93 controls organized into 14 control domains. Organizations must conduct risk assessment, select appropriate controls from Annex A or implement alternative controls, document the ISMS, and demonstrate continual improvement. ISO27001 certification requires independent audit by a certification body. Key concepts include: ISMS, risk-based approach, PDCA cycle, statement of applicability (SOA), risk treatment plan, information security objectives, continual improvement, and management commitment.

roles.description: Responsible for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving the ISMS in accordance with ISO27001.
roles.detailed_knowledge: The Information Security Manager is responsible for the overall ISMS and ISO27001 compliance. This role requires deep understanding of ISO27001 requirements, risk management, information security controls, and management systems. Key activities include establishing the ISMS scope and boundaries, conducting information security risk assessments, developing risk treatment plans, selecting and implementing controls from Annex A, developing ISMS documentation (policies, procedures, processes), establishing information security objectives, coordinating internal audits, managing management reviews, overseeing corrective actions, ensuring continual improvement, and coordinating certification audits. The manager must understand the PDCA cycle, risk-based approach, Annex A controls, statement of applicability (SOA), and continual improvement requirements. They work with top management to ensure leadership commitment, with business units to understand context and requirements, with IT to implement technical controls, and with all stakeholders to ensure awareness and compliance. The role requires strong project management, risk management, and communication skills. Evidence of effective performance includes a well-documented ISMS, effective risk management, implemented controls, successful internal audits, management review records, and certification achievement.
key_questions: What is the scope of our ISMS?
How do we conduct information security risk assessment?
What controls should we implement from Annex A?
What is our statement of applicability?
How do we ensure continual improvement?

roles.description: Conducts information security risk assessments and develops risk treatment plans in accordance with ISO27001 requirements.
roles.detailed_knowledge: The Risk Management Specialist conducts information security risk assessments, which are fundamental to ISO27001. The standard requires organizations to identify information security risks and implement appropriate controls. Key activities include establishing risk assessment methodology, identifying information assets, identifying threats and vulnerabilities, assessing risk likelihood and impact, evaluating existing controls, calculating risk levels, developing risk treatment plans, selecting controls from Annex A or implementing alternative controls, documenting risk assessment and treatment, and reviewing risks regularly. The specialist must understand ISO27001 risk assessment requirements, use appropriate risk assessment methodologies, consider information security risks to confidentiality, integrity, and availability, and document risk assessment thoroughly. They work with the Information Security Manager, business units, and IT to understand assets, threats, and vulnerabilities. The role requires expertise in risk assessment methodologies, information security, and ISO27001 requirements. Evidence of effective performance includes comprehensive risk assessment reports, risk registers, risk treatment plans, and documented risk management processes.
responsibilities: Identify information assets, threats, and vulnerabilities
key_questions: How do we conduct information security risk assessment?
What information assets do we have?
What threats and vulnerabilities exist?
What is our risk level?
What controls should we implement?

roles.description: Conducts internal audits of the ISMS to verify conformity with ISO27001 requirements and ISMS effectiveness.
roles.detailed_knowledge: The Internal Auditor conducts internal audits of the ISMS to verify conformity with ISO27001 requirements and the effectiveness of the ISMS. Internal audits are required by ISO27001 and must be conducted at planned intervals. Key activities include developing audit plans, conducting audit activities (opening meeting, document review, interviews, observations, testing), documenting audit findings, preparing audit reports, following up on corrective actions, and maintaining audit records. The auditor must understand ISO27001 requirements, audit principles, audit techniques, and the organization's ISMS. They must be independent and objective, and should not audit their own work. The auditor evaluates: ISMS conformity with ISO27001 requirements, ISMS effectiveness, implementation of controls, risk management effectiveness, and continual improvement. Audit findings may include nonconformities (failure to meet requirements), opportunities for improvement, and positive observations. The auditor works with the Information Security Manager and auditees to conduct audits and follow up on findings. Evidence of effective auditing includes comprehensive audit plans, audit reports, audit findings, and follow-up records.
key_questions: How do we plan internal audits?
What audit techniques should we use?
How do we document audit findings?
What constitutes a nonconformity?
How do we follow up on audit findings?

roles.description: Responsible for implementing, operating, and maintaining specific information security controls within the ISMS.
roles.detailed_knowledge: Control Owners are responsible for implementing, operating, and maintaining specific information security controls. Each control from Annex A or alternative control should have an assigned owner. Key activities include understanding control requirements, implementing controls according to risk treatment plans, operating controls effectively, monitoring control effectiveness, maintaining control documentation, reporting control status, participating in internal audits, and implementing corrective actions. Control owners must understand their assigned controls, how controls address risks, control implementation requirements, and control operation procedures. They work with the Information Security Manager to understand requirements, with risk management to understand risk context, and with internal auditors during audits. The role requires understanding of information security, assigned controls, and operational procedures. Evidence of effective control ownership includes implemented controls, control documentation, control monitoring records, and positive audit findings.
key_questions: What are the requirements for my assigned control?
How do I implement this control?
How do I operate this control?
How do I monitor control effectiveness?
What documentation is needed?

use_cases.name: ISMS Establishment and Implementation
use_cases.description: Establishes and implements an ISMS in accordance with ISO27001 requirements, including scope definition, risk assessment, and control implementation.
use_cases.detailed_knowledge: ISMS Establishment and Implementation is the foundation of ISO27001 compliance. This use case covers: understanding organizational context (internal and external issues, interested parties), defining ISMS scope and boundaries, conducting information security risk assessment, developing risk treatment plan, selecting controls from Annex A or implementing alternative controls, developing statement of applicability (SOA), establishing information security policy and objectives, developing ISMS documentation (policies, procedures, processes), implementing selected controls, establishing roles and responsibilities, providing resources and competence, ensuring awareness and communication, and establishing operational processes. The ISMS must be established following the PDCA cycle: Plan (establish ISMS, risk assessment, risk treatment), Do (implement and operate controls), Check (monitor, measure, audit, review), Act (maintain and improve). Effective ISMS establishment requires top management commitment, adequate resources, risk-based approach, and comprehensive documentation. The ISMS should be integrated into organizational processes and aligned with business objectives.
use_cases.business_value: Establishes a systematic approach to information security management, enabling risk-based security, compliance with ISO27001, and continual improvement.
example_queries: How do we establish an ISMS?
What is the scope of our ISMS?
How do we conduct information security risk assessment?
What is a statement of applicability?

use_cases.name: Information Security Risk Assessment
use_cases.description: Conducts comprehensive information security risk assessment to identify risks and determine risk treatment.
use_cases.detailed_knowledge: Information Security Risk Assessment is a core requirement of ISO27001. The standard requires organizations to identify information security risks and implement appropriate controls. Key components include: establishing risk assessment methodology, identifying information assets (data, systems, processes, people), identifying threats (natural, human, technical, environmental), identifying vulnerabilities (system, process, physical), assessing risk likelihood and impact, evaluating existing controls, calculating risk levels, determining risk treatment options (avoid, mitigate, transfer, accept), developing risk treatment plan, selecting controls from Annex A or implementing alternative controls, and documenting risk assessment. Risk assessment should consider risks to confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information. The risk assessment methodology should be documented and consistently applied. Risk assessment should be conducted regularly and when significant changes occur. Effective risk assessment enables informed decision-making about security investments and control selection.
use_cases.business_value: Enables risk-based information security management, identifies security risks, and supports informed control selection and risk treatment decisions.
example_queries: How do we conduct information security risk assessment?
What information assets do we have?
What risk treatment options are available?

use_cases.name: Control Selection and Implementation
use_cases.description: Selects appropriate controls from Annex A or implements alternative controls based on risk treatment plans.
use_cases.detailed_knowledge: Control Selection and Implementation involves selecting and implementing information security controls based on risk treatment plans. ISO27001 Annex A provides 93 controls organized into 14 control domains: A.5 Information security policies, A.6 Organization of information security, A.7 Human resource security, A.8 Asset management, A.9 Access control, A.10 Cryptography, A.11 Physical and environmental security, A.12 Operations security, A.13 Communications security, A.14 System acquisition development and maintenance, A.15 Supplier relationships, A.16 Information security incident management, A.17 Information security aspects of business continuity management, A.18 Compliance. Organizations must select controls based on risk treatment plans. Controls can be selected from Annex A or alternative controls can be implemented. The statement of applicability (SOA) documents which controls are selected and why. Control implementation requires: understanding control requirements, developing implementation plans, implementing controls, documenting controls, operating controls, monitoring control effectiveness, and maintaining controls. Effective control implementation requires clear ownership, adequate resources, proper documentation, and ongoing monitoring.
use_cases.business_value: Implements appropriate security controls to address identified risks, ensuring effective information security and ISO27001 compliance.
example_queries: What controls should we implement from Annex A?
What is a statement of applicability?
What alternative controls can we use?

annex_a_control_domains.detailed_knowledge: A.5 focuses on establishing management direction and support for information security through policies. Key controls include: A.5.1.1 Policies for information security (developing, reviewing, approving information security policies), A.5.1.2 Review of policies for information security (reviewing policies at planned intervals). Information security policies should be approved by management, published, communicated to relevant personnel, and reviewed regularly. Policies should be aligned with business objectives and legal/regulatory requirements.

annex_a_control_domains.description: Ensuring authorized user access and preventing unauthorized access to information systems.
annex_a_control_domains.detailed_knowledge: A.9 focuses on access control to ensure authorized access and prevent unauthorized access. Key control areas include: A.9.1 Business requirements of access control (access control policy, access to networks and network services), A.9.2 User access management (user registration and de-registration, user access provisioning, management of privileged access rights, management of secret authentication information, review of user access rights, removal or adjustment of access rights), A.9.3 User responsibilities (use of secret authentication information), A.9.4 System and application access control (information access restriction, secure log-on procedures, password management system, use of privileged utility programs, access control to program source code). Access control should be based on business requirements, least privilege principles, and regular access reviews.

annex_a_control_domains.description: Protection of information processing facilities and systems.
annex_a_control_domains.detailed_knowledge: A.12 focuses on operations security to protect information processing facilities and systems. Key control areas include: A.12.1 Operational procedures and responsibilities (documented operating procedures, change management, capacity management, separation of development testing and operational environments), A.12.2 Protection from malware (controls against malware), A.12.3 Backup (information backup), A.12.4 Logging and monitoring (event logging, protection of log information, administrator and operator logs, clock synchronization), A.12.5 Control of operational software (installation of software on operational systems), A.12.6 Technical vulnerability management (management of technical vulnerabilities, restrictions on software installation), A.12.7 Information systems audit considerations (information systems audit controls). Operations security ensures secure and reliable operation of information systems.

common_control_areas.description: Conducting information security risk assessment and implementing risk treatment plans.
common_control_areas.detailed_knowledge: Risk Assessment and Treatment is fundamental to ISO27001. The standard requires organizations to identify information security risks and implement appropriate controls. Key components include: establishing risk assessment methodology, identifying information assets, identifying threats and vulnerabilities, assessing risk likelihood and impact, evaluating existing controls, calculating risk levels, determining risk treatment options (avoid, mitigate, transfer, accept), developing risk treatment plans, selecting controls from Annex A or implementing alternative controls, and documenting risk assessment and treatment. Risk assessment should be conducted regularly and when significant changes occur. Risk treatment should address identified risks through appropriate controls. Effective risk assessment and treatment enables risk-based information security management.
key_questions: How do we conduct information security risk assessment?
What information assets do we have?
What threats and vulnerabilities exist?
What risk treatment options are available?
What controls should we implement?

common_control_areas.description: Developing and maintaining ISMS documentation in accordance with ISO27001 requirements.
common_control_areas.detailed_knowledge: ISMS Documentation is required by ISO27001. The standard requires documentation of: ISMS scope, information security policy, information security risk assessment process, risk treatment process, information security objectives, statement of applicability (SOA), control documentation, and other documents necessary for ISMS effectiveness. Documentation should be controlled, reviewed, updated, and available to relevant personnel. Effective ISMS documentation provides evidence of ISMS implementation, supports ISMS operation, enables continual improvement, and supports certification audits.
key_questions: What ISMS documentation is required?
How do we document the ISMS?
How do we control ISMS documentation?
What is a statement of applicability?
How do we maintain ISMS documentation?

domain_knowledge_documents.content: ISO27001 requires organizations to conduct information security risk assessment. The risk assessment methodology should be documented and consistently applied. Key steps include: establishing risk assessment methodology (defining risk assessment approach, risk criteria, risk scales), identifying information assets (data, systems, processes, people, facilities), identifying threats (natural disasters, human threats, technical failures, environmental threats), identifying vulnerabilities (system vulnerabilities, process weaknesses, physical vulnerabilities), assessing risk likelihood (probability of threat exploiting vulnerability), assessing risk impact (consequences to confidentiality, integrity, availability), evaluating existing controls (effectiveness of current security measures), calculating risk levels (combining likelihood and impact), determining risk treatment options (avoid, mitigate, transfer, accept), developing risk treatment plans (selecting controls, assigning responsibilities, setting timelines), and documenting risk assessment. Risk assessment should consider risks to confidentiality (unauthorized disclosure), integrity (unauthorized modification), and availability (loss of access). The risk assessment should be conducted regularly and when significant changes occur. Documentation should be comprehensive and maintained as evidence of risk management.

The Statement of Applicability (SOA) is a required document in ISO27001 that lists all controls from Annex A, indicates whether each control is implemented or not, and provides justification for the decision. The SOA should include: list of all Annex A controls, implementation status (implemented, not implemented, partially implemented), justification for implementation decisions (based on risk assessment, risk treatment plan), reference to risk treatment plan, and any alternative controls implemented. The SOA demonstrates that control selection is based on risk assessment and risk treatment. It should be reviewed and updated when risks change or controls are modified. The SOA is a key document for certification audits and demonstrates systematic approach to control selection.
